title: Oscars 2026: 'Sinners' wins Best Original Screenplay
text: 'Sinners' made Academy Awards history at the 2026 ceremony, though not in the way anticipated. With 16 nominations—more than any film ever—it was expected to dominate the awards. Instead, it set a different record: the most Oscar losses, with 12 defeats against only four victories. The Four Winners: 'Sinners' triumphed in these categories: Best Actor: Michael B. Jordan; Best Director: Ryan Coogler (the first Black director to win this award); Best Cinematography: Autumn Durald Arkapaw (the first woman to win in this category); Best Original Screenplay: Ryan Coogler. Expectations vs. Reality: Despite winning major precursor awards like the Screen Actors Guild Awards, the film lost in key categories including Best Picture, Best Film Editing, and casting (a newly created category). As one analysis noted, the preferential ballot system may favor "depth of their passions than the breadth of their respect," suggesting 'Sinners' likely finished close second in many categories. The article emphasizes that while the film didn't achieve the predicted sweep, its cultural and artistic impact—and Coogler's groundbreaking ownership deal—represents historic significance beyond Oscar tallies.
